changes.reason: Batch A call-wall realization test. The worst MAE is call-wall specific and tied to an unusually wide realized target; lowering the profile dynamic cap from 6 to 5 retests the only prior call-wall TP adjustment that was effectively flat-to-positive versus the leader while reducing target convexity pressure.

Compress the call-wall base realization distance from the current wider effective target regime toward the grid midpoint. This is intended to monetize accepted call-wall moves faster without changing admission gates or touching protected pullback/charm/squeeze profiles.

Require sustained strike acceptance for 60 seconds so the smaller realization box is paired with stronger persistence rather than simply taking faster exits on one-print acceptance. This keeps the call-wall concept intact and avoids global veto tightening.

expected_effect: Should reduce call_wall_acceptance_long hold time, exposure, and hidden MAE pressure from the 120-minute convex recovery while preserving most of the profile's positive expectancy. Tradeoff is possible underpayment of the rare $546 call-wall winner; reject if total net profit drops more than 2% versus run_001/run_044, call-wall PnL retention is poor without MAE improvement, or pnl_per_exposure_hour falls.
label: call-wall-realization-persistence-box
rationale: Use Batch A only. The current portfolio edge is broad and should be protected; the only unresolved portfolio tail is call_wall_acceptance_long, which has just 2 VALID_0DTE trades, $640.5 PnL, strong $290/hour efficiency, but owns the -655.2 worst MAE and a 120-minute recovery path. Broad filters and setup floors would destroy protected PnL, while prior call-wall admission loosening was mostly sample-building. This experiment changes realization and persistence for the call-wall profile without touching global vetoes, no-0DTE routing, or the protected pullback/charm-pin/squeeze edges.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long contributes $640.5 on only 2 trades and owns the -655.2 worst MAE; the worst trade still made $546 after early favorable movement and later deep heat, indicating a convex recovery state rather than a simple bad entry.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a two-trade profile and cut rare convex winners unless validated against protected fast-winner retention and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long entry with wide target or extended ES-VWAP that fails to make sustained early progress before adverse expansion.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE below -655.2 toward <= -513.4 while retaining >=70% call-wall PnL, total net profit >=98% of leader, pnl_per_exposure_hour flat or better, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes 20 trades and $1315.3 with no slow trades, but direct 1DTE tightening has repeatedly been risky. A separate pullback companion may improve 1DTE MAE without weakening breakout_long_1dte.
strategy_hypotheses.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The companion may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable under current trigger logic.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL stays >= $1315, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -124.6, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, and total risk-adjusted score is flat or better.
